free_text: Neutron were detected in a small Li6I(Eu)
scintillator placed at the centre of polyethylene
moderating spheres with sizes ranging from 5 cm to
30 cm in diameter. The counter was positioned at 20 cm
from the target when using the 5.1 cm and 7.6 cm
diameter polyethylene spheres, and at 40 cm when using
the larger spheres. In addition, measurements were made
for each sphere at 100 cm from the target to determine
The targets used were either monoisotopic or
isotopically enriched to greater than 90% and were
deposited on 0.020 inch thick gold discs.
No explanation of relative units is given. The angular
distribution for In-115 was isotropic to within 5%.
The 12.7 cm diameter counter at 40 cm from the target

The 7.6 cm diameter counter was used. This counter
was positioned at 0 degr. to the proton beam and
subtended a half angle of 45 degr. from the target.
In addition a long counter was positioned directly
behind the 7.6cm counter at the same solid angle.
